rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 16, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Deere & Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Stuckart was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GENE LEPORE VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 3. Defendant Deere & Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Lepore was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories (defendant¿s Exhibit S) and the deposition testimony of Mr. Bangs, Mr. Bailey, Mr. Villegas and Mr. Roman preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant¿s moving papers failed to properly make an issue of substantial factor causation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 5. Defendant Dillingham Construction Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Patterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid substantive responses, as opposed to only objections, to contention discovery designed to elicit all of plaintiffs¿ evidence in support of their claims against defendant. Defendant did not attempt to sustain its initial burden under the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 5. Defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals¿ motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the claim for loss of consortium and granted as to the claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Patterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Patterson¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B & P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative is not contested, the prevailing party is required to bring the proposed order to the hearing or sumbit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
